vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is not visible. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is absent; the white rabbit anchor is present but the plant is not.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: Pothos absent; only the white rabbit anchor remains.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver guest foliage.

plant_audit.P2_P4_Black_Pot: Contains a single, thin, etiolated green sprout/stem. Likely a failed propagation or weed.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Contains white rabbit anchor; soil appears undisturbed and devoid of foliage.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, damp, and potentially compacted.
biome_observations.debris: White fragments (likely eggshell or perlite) present in the black pot containing the rabbit anchor.
biome_observations.anomalies: Introduction of a white cylindrical object (possibly a sensor or marker) in the current image near the yellow pot.

temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Introduction of a metallic spoon near the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Loss of visibility due to lighting; spoon removed.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Introduction of a white cylindrical object (sensor/marker) in the yellow pot area.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of 'Biological Stasis' or 'Systemic Failure'. There is no active, healthy plant life visible in any of the pots. The environment appears to be a testing ground for soil or sensor calibration rather than a functional botanical display.
vision_report.narrative_description: My audit began by establishing the baseline registry against the provided image stack. I first performed a visual scan of each pot across the timeline to identify any signs of life. After validating my findings, I concluded that the registry does not match the physical reality. The pots are essentially empty of the specified flora. The presence of a white rabbit anchor and various non-botanical objects (spoon, sensor) suggests this is a controlled experimental setup rather than a traditional garden. The plants are either dead, removed, or never established.
